issues: 1x silhouette noisy — fire sparks fragment the outline and dissolve readable shape
charm is absent: rectangular scroll with angular fire reads functional/utilitarian, not soft/toy-like
fire elements too small at 1x to register as fire; look like stray pixels
dark-bg contrast marginal — shadow underside and dark-outline scroll body merge at 4x DARK
no round/soft forms; design skews edgy-pixel rather than friendly-sticker
reasoning: The scroll body is recognisable at 4x on both light and dark backgrounds, and the orange fire accent provides colour pop. However the core Cute & Charm criteria are not met: silhouette is broken up by small angular flame sparks that add noise without adding personality, the overall form is boxy and stiff rather than round and huggable, and the 1x read is a blurry rectangle with ambiguous speckles. On the dark panel the scroll merges with the background shadow, reducing confidence further. Passes a bare minimum legibility bar (hence 5 not ≤4) but fails the sticker test — you would not instantly read 'charming fire-scroll' from the 1x thumbnail. Needs softer silhouette, rounder rolled-scroll ends, and larger/cleaner flame shape to push into pass territory.
